description: Tests the topological interpretation of polar-time holonomy: pi_1(M) = Z. 6 circuits covering winding number linearity, shape invariance, speed invariance, and sign reversal. The conjecture is topological, not geometric: flat space (R=0), non-trivial fundamental group.
falsification: FALSIFIED if: (1) shape deformation changes phase (-> geometric, not topological), (2) phase does not scale linearly with n, or (3) phase is zero across all n (-> wrong theta_t / Bloch equator identification).
connection_to_v3: GPT-2 v3 found shape-invariant, schedule-invariant, orientation-reversing holonomy in CP^15 (polar_holonomy_v3_results.md). That is the representational analogue of what this suite tests physically. Cross-substrate confirmation would strongly support the conjecture.
next_design_instruction: Design winding_n1 first (establish Phi_0), then winding_n1_shape_deformed (critical falsification test), then winding_n2/n3 (linearity). Run all in the same IBM session where possible to cancel systematic hardware errors.

circuits.hypothesis: 1 full equatorial winding. If polar-time topology is real, P(0) shifts from 0.5 by hardware-independent Phi_0. Baseline for all linearity comparisons.
2 windings -> phase 2*Phi_0. Topological: doubles exactly. Geometric: scales with area. Linearity test.
3 windings -> 3*Phi_0. Linear scaling is the topological signature.
Reverse direction -> phase -Phi_0. Exact sign reversal required. Distinguishes topology from decoherence.
Elliptical path (alternating large/small Rz steps). TOPOLOGICAL: same phase as winding_n1. GEOMETRIC: different phase. THIS IS THE CRITICAL DISTINGUISHING TEST.
4x slower traversal (32 Rz steps instead of 8). TOPOLOGICAL: same phase. DECOHERENCE: more noise. Tests schedule invariance found in GPT-2 v3 experiment.
